header.headline: RoPE layouts compared
header.subtitle: The board shows that both paths share the same angle schedule but rotate across different channel planes.

legend.title: Shared frame
legend.note: This family explains compatibility choices, not free-form prose.

left.title: Pairwise rotation
left.subtitle: Even and odd channels form the 2D rotation plane for each local pair.
bullets: Checkpoint family defines the expected channel pairing
llama.cpp compatibility depends on matching this layout
Wrong layout diverges immediately after RoPE
left.note: Use this when the training stack already defines pairwise planes.

center.title: Split-half rotation
center.subtitle: The first half of the vector rotates against the second half instead of even-odd pairs.
bullets: The angle schedule can stay identical while planes differ
Checkpoint semantics still change because the channel contract changes
Interoperability breaks if a runtime assumes the wrong plane
center.note: Use this only when the checkpoint family was trained on split-half RoPE.

right.title: Treat layout as semantics
right.subtitle: RoPE layout is a compatibility rule inherited from training, templates, and reference runtimes.
bullets: Do not treat layout choice as a tuning knob
Choose the path that matches the checkpoint family
Check hidden-state parity before blaming later kernels
right.note: The board should make the compatibility rule obvious at a glance.

metric_1.label: Rotation plane
metric_1.detail: The plane definition is the core comparison

metric_2.label: First divergence
metric_2.detail: A mismatch shows up before later attention math

callout_1.title: Use pairwise when the checkpoint family demands it
callout_1.body: Llama-derived stacks and llama.cpp-compatible checkpoints expect consecutive pairs.

callout_2.title: Use split-half when the model was trained that way
callout_2.body: NeoX-style implementations rotate the first half against the second half and checkpoints inherit that semantic choice.
lines: Runtime must preserve the same layout

callout_3.title: Stop at parity, not at intuition
callout_3.body: If the hidden state drifts after RoPE, fix the layout contract before changing anything downstream.

footer.note: The successor board family should make compatibility rules legible while keeping all topic-specific wording in payload files.
